provenance.source_corpus: Influence of Acid Mine Drainage, and Its Remediation, on Lakewater Quality and Benthic Invertebrate Communities

source_spans.text: Restoration of the site was undertaken in 2008 and completed in 2010.
Between these years, lakewater pH increased by about one unit
concentrations of many trace metals declined substantially

nodes.name: populations of an acid-tolerant Chironomus species

some new taxa appeared (Trichoptera, Odonata and the Ceratopogonidae Bezzia)
Acid mine drainage (AMD) results when water, oxygen and microorganisms oxidize pyrite ore

edges.original_sentence: Following remediation, in 2011, there was a strong decrease in the concentrations of most major ions and trace metals (e.g., Fe: -90%) and pH values had increased by 0.75 to 1.79 units at stations in Lake Arnoux.

edges.original_sentence: Acid mine drainage (AMD) results when water, oxygen and microorganisms oxidize pyrite ore (FeS2) in groundwater (Massmann et al. 2003) and in mine tailings (D. B. Johnson et al. 2005) , thereby releasing dissolved metals in highly acidic solution
